## Problem Statement

Enterprise customers with 50+ users cannot adopt ProductPilot OS organization-wide because the product lacks SAML 2.0 SSO integration and SCIM automated provisioning. IT security policies at these companies prohibit deployment of SaaS products without centralized identity management. As a result, enterprise adoption is limited to the individual PM team that manually manages credentials — blocking multi-team expansion and in 3 cases blocking the initial enterprise purchase entirely.

**Who is affected:** Enterprise accounts with IT-managed identity providers (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace). Approximately 40–60 of the current and pipeline enterprise accounts meet this profile.
**Severity:** Deal-blocking in new sales; expansion-blocking in existing accounts.
**Current workaround:** IT departments prohibit rollout beyond the initial PM team; some accounts use shared admin credentials (a security violation they want to eliminate).

---

| 2 | Gong Call | Enterprise eval | TechCorp IT Director | 2024-05-07 | "We can't deploy to our 200-person ops team without SSO. Our security policy is non-negotiable. We deploy everything through Okta or we don't deploy it." | High |
| 3 | Zendesk #21043 | Support — expansion blocked | Meridian Group ($340K) | 2024-04-28 | "How do we integrate ProductPilot with our Okta instance? We've tried the workaround with individual accounts but our IT team flagged it as a policy violation." | High |
| 4 | Salesforce Opportunity | Deal blocker | AE: Marcus Webb — GlobalCorp ($680K) | 2024-04-15 | "GlobalCorp CPO wants to expand from 5 PMs to 40 PMs. IT is blocking it — no SSO, no enterprise rollout. This is a $680K opportunity we could close in Q3." | High |
| 5 | Zendesk #19872 | Feature request | DataCore Inc. ($195K) | 2024-04-10 | "We need SCIM provisioning to automate user management at scale. Onboarding 20 new PMs through manual credential setup is not acceptable for an enterprise purchase." | Medium |
| 6 | Gong Call | Lost deal analysis | Former prospect — DataPipeline Co. | 2024-03-28 | "We went with [Competitor] primarily because they had SSO. It wasn't close. ProductPilot was a better product but SSO was non-negotiable for our security team." | High |
| 7 | Slack #cs-escalations | CS escalation | CSM: @aisha.okonkwo | 2024-05-12 | "Apex Analytics is asking about SSO again. This is the third time in 6 months. If we don't have it by Q3, I'm worried about their renewal." | Medium |
| 8 | Salesforce — Lost Deal | Deal lost | AE: James Park — NovaCorp | 2024-04-20 | "Lost NovaCorp to Competitor X. Security requirements: SSO mandatory. Post-loss note: they said they'd reconsider if we add SSO in next 6 months." | High |

## Customer Impact

**Affected customer count (estimated):** 40–60 accounts (current + pipeline with enterprise IT requirements)
**Affected customer segments:** Enterprise accounts (50+ user deployments), particularly those in regulated industries (financial services, healthcare, government contractors)
**Impact severity:** Deal-blocking (new sales) and expansion-blocking (existing accounts)

**Representative customer quotes:**

> "We can't deploy to our 200-person ops team without SSO. Our security policy is non-negotiable. We deploy everything through Okta or we don't deploy it."
> — IT Director, TechCorp (Enterprise, $420K ACV deal), Gong call 2024-05-07

> "We went with [Competitor] primarily because they had SSO. It wasn't close. ProductPilot was a better product but SSO was non-negotiable for our security team."
> — Former prospect, DataPipeline Co., Gong call 2024-03-28

> "We need SCIM provisioning to automate user management at scale. Onboarding 20 new PMs through manual credential setup is not acceptable for an enterprise purchase."
> — DataCore Inc. ($195K ACV), Zendesk #19872

## Strategic Alignment

**OKR alignment score:** 9/10
**Q2 OKR:** "Win 5 enterprise deals >$200K ACV" — this directly affects 3 deals meeting this criteria
**Q3 OKR:** "Reduce enterprise churn to <5%" — Apex Analytics is a near-term churn risk

**Competitive context:** Both Competitor X and Competitor Y have SAML SSO and SCIM provisioning. This is not a differentiator — it is a table-stakes requirement for enterprise. ProductPilot OS is at a structural competitive disadvantage in enterprise sales until this ships.

---

## Technical Complexity

**Engineering complexity estimate:** Medium
**Estimated effort:** 1 sprint (SSO) + 1 sprint (SCIM) = 2 sprints total, 3–4 engineers
**Key dependencies:** Identity provider testing (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace)
**Technical risk:** Low — SAML 2.0 and SCIM are well-established standards with extensive library support

*Engineering note: SAML implementation using existing open-source libraries (e.g., passport-saml for Node.js or python-saml). SCIM 2.0 endpoint implementation is additive — no core data model changes required.*

## Recommended Next Step

**Recommendation:** ✅ Prioritize Now — P1

**Rationale:** The evidence is the strongest I've reviewed in the last 2 quarters: 8 signals from 5 source types, $2.1M in confirmed blocked ARR, 2 deals already lost with SSO cited. Engineering complexity is low (standard protocols, well-supported libraries). This is a table-stakes enterprise requirement that is actively costing revenue.

**Suggested placement:** P1, elevating above the current P1 (API performance improvement — score: 5,100) given the significantly higher business impact.
